oneLiner: Protein without heaviness: the functional, refreshing way to hit your protein target.
positioningStatement: PEP is a premium protein drink designed for disciplined, health-oriented people who want to conveniently reach their protein goals with a light, refreshing taste. It’s a functional nutrition tool optimised for efficiency, consumed mostly alone or as part of a wellness routine — not a gym-bro shake, not a lifestyle treat — but a rational, enjoyable choice for busy, health-focused achievers.
audience: Active professionals aiming to hit protein targets
People focused on efficiency and nutrition without the heaviness of shakes
occasions: At work for a functional break

visual.mood: Clean and fresh with a highly functional, purposeful feel. Nutrition front and center.
visual.photography: High energy, but solo or gym context: active people, clean backgrounds, gym bags, working desks, clean city or nature. Never bar, party, brunch, or staged lifestyle moments.
visual.typography: Didot for hero (premium/clean), Montserrat for info overlays, Bebas Neue for badges. Script (Brittany Signature) only for tagline/slogan, never headlines. No display cursives in product info.

inLineBrands.name: HOLY
inLineBrands.why: Functional layout with clear vitamin/nutrition benefits, supports a disciplined and health-oriented audience.

inLineBrands.name: LMNT
inLineBrands.why: Directly targets hydration and nutrition for athletic/professional routines, packaging is clinical and efficient.

inLineBrands.name: NOCCO
inLineBrands.why: Focuses on sports routines and efficiency, functional performance-driven branding, not a lifestyle/party vibe.

inLineBrands.name: VITAMIN WELL
inLineBrands.why: Functional drink, clean and transparent packaging, focuses on active hydration and daily nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Bloom is positioned around prebiotics and a playful, wellness lifestyle treat, not the disciplined, functional, protein-oriented use-case PEP targets.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Ever Tree prioritises social and lifestyle moments (alcohol-free aperitif), not practical, solo, functional routines or protein goals.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Olipop has a wellness/lifestyle positioning focusing on gut health and soft branding, not the disciplined, optimization-driven nutrition use-case.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic soda, positioned for wellness lifestyle and daily health, not as a functional tool for disciplined protein hitting.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Lifestyle kombucha with a wellness and artisan vibe — not primarily about functional efficiency or protein.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Infused sparkling water for relaxation and lifestyle break, not a protein-centric, performance-driven product.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: RTD cocktails/mocktails; occasion and communication are social/lifestyle, not about daily functional nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Alcohol-free spirits and mocktails for mindful social occasions; irrelevant to functional solo/fitness routine moments.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Social drinks/mocktails for mood enhancement and gatherings — not about solo, routine-driven functional efficiency.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Non-alcoholic spirits focus on social ritual and replacements for spirits in groups, not individual routine-driven nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium, social non-alcoholic drinks consumed like an aperitif — communication and ritual are not about functional protein goals.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Overtly macho, 'bro', or aggressive performance language, unapproachable and intimidating to broader disciplined audiences.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com (US)
antiReferences.why: Name collision, completely different product (energy/probiotic), much more casual and social — can confuse market.

antiReferences.name: Party mocktails/Brunch sodas
antiReferences.why: Emphasise indulgence and entertainment over daily performance and discipline.

antiReferences.name: Kids' juice drinks
antiReferences.why: Fun, overly sweet, childish branding; lacks functional or efficiency-driven proposition.

brandFitRules: Brand must signal functional benefit (protein amount, nutrition) up-front
Visuals should feel clean, efficient, and rational, never playful or indulgent
No design tropes from gym-bro, aggressive fitness, or social/party/lifestyle drinks
Color palette and typography must match brand.json: bold serif, clear sans-serif, orange/green/cream/white
Occasion in communication must be solo, routine-driven, or practical, not social/brunch/bar moments
Do not use mocktail or cocktail language, or social ritual cues
Photography must focus on active, disciplined individuals, not social groups
Tagline should be optimistic but grounded ('Feel good. Have fun.') without hyped-up or extreme language
